THE FINAL WEIGHT OF THE FRAME, $ W_f $, CAN BE EXPRESSED AS $ W_f = W \times (1 - 0.02)^n $, WHERE $ W $ IS THE BASE WEIGHT OF THE FRAME AND $ N $ IS THE NUMBER OF ADVANCED COMPONENTS ADDED. THIS EQUATION ACCOUNTS FOR THE 2% WEIGHT REDUCTION PER COMPONENT.

TO ACHIEVE A TOTAL REDUCTION OF ENERGY EXPENDITURE BY 25%, THE FUNCTION $ E(n) = 1 - (1 - 0.02)^n $ MUST EQUAL 0.25. SOLVING FOR $ N $, WE GET $ 0.25 = 1 - (0.98)^n $, WHICH SIMPLIFIES TO $ (0.98)^n = 0.75 $. TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES, $ N \times \ln(0.98) = \ln(0.75) $. SOLVING FOR $ N $, WE FIND $ N = \ln(0.75) / \ln(0.98) \approx 13.51 $. SINCE $ N $ MUST BE AN INTEGER, THE MINIMUM NUMBER OF ADVANCED COMPONENTS REQUIRED IS 14.

THE CLOCK TICKS FORWARD AS TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE IN PREPARING FOR THE EVENT. THE CLOCK CONTINUES TO MEASURE THE PROGRESS MADE IN OPTIMIZING THE BIKE'S PERFORMANCE. THE CLOCK REMINDS US THAT EVERY MOMENT COUNTS WHEN ACHIEVING THE DESIRED ENERGY REDUCTION.
